pages.full_text:
102 August 4th.
    We drove down to Glouces[ter]
to drop Peter off at music camp, putting
up his tent. Then dropped off Simon's
'cello to be mended

August 10th.
    Kate Warburton has bravely come
with us. There were huge jams on
the M5. Kate babysat John, Charles &
William while Simon, Eve & Thomas
heard Peter sing & play his violin in the
orchestra at Beauchamp House.
    We picked up Simon's 'cello, &
drove on to __St. Augustine's__ working farm
__Arlingham__.
    Peter & John slept in the [?bukta] &
Kate slept on the bottom bunk. There
was a playground with an old tractor
in it for the boys to climb on, & a half-
pages.notes: Page number 102 visible top-left. The word after 'slept in the' is unclear — possibly 'bukta' (a type of sleeping shelter/bag). 'Glouces' is cut off at the right margin but clearly Gloucester. Year not stated on this page; based on volume position (1989–2003, pdf_page 53 of 350) the year is uncertain — possibly early 1990s.

pages.full_text:
Chinese pig & hoardes of rabbits &
other animals.
Aug 11th. The next day we went for a
walk 3 miles, instead of church,
because the service wasn't until 11.
    The River Severn has
    scoured a great loop
    around the village & fields.
After lunch we dropped Thomas off
at __Beauchamp__ & set of for B'ham.

August 18th.
    Eve drove 5 boys & [Tony]
nanny to
Thorpeness. We arrived & had a
terrible time putting up the tent.
__John's birthday__
    John zigzagged up the
theare in a row boat with Tony
got tossed about in the sea in his
pages.route_sketch_description: Small hand-drawn sketch of a river loop labelled 'R. Severn' with an arrow showing the river winding around a loop, illustrating the text 'The River Severn has scoured a great loop around the village & fields.'
pages.notes: Page number 103 visible top-right. The word 'Tony' appears as a superscript annotation above 'nanny'. 'hoardes' is the diary's spelling (not corrected). 'theare' may be a misspelling of 'there' or possibly a place name — left as written. 'B'ham' is an abbreviation for Birmingham. Text continues beyond the bottom of the page — the spread is cut off mid-sentence. Year not explicitly stated; same trip as left page.
